intitule: School Life and Authorities: Vocabulary Quiz (Ressource)

enonce.texte: Read the sentences below and choose the correct word related to school life and authorities.

questions.texte: The _______ is responsible for the overall management of the school, including discipline and academic affairs.

sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect. The bursar manages finances.
Incorrect. The discipline master focuses only on student behavior.
Correct. The principal is the head of the school.
Incorrect. The secretary handles administrative tasks and calls.

questions.texte: If you want to pay your school fees, you should go to the _______ office.

sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect. The principal does not directly collect fees.
Correct. The bursar is the financial officer of the school.
Incorrect. The staff room is for teachers.
Incorrect. The library is for reading and studying.

questions.texte: Students caught fighting may be sent to the _______ for punishment.

sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect. The classroom is for learning.
Incorrect. The laboratory is for science experiments.
Correct. The discipline master handles student misbehavior.
Incorrect. The playground is for recreation.

questions.texte: The list of courses offered this term is called the _______.

sousQuestions.reponse: Correct. The timetable shows the schedule of subjects.
Incorrect. A report card shows student grades.
Incorrect. A syllabus outlines the course content, but the 'timetable' is the schedule.
Incorrect. An admission letter confirms enrollment.

questions.texte: The _______ answers phone calls, types letters, and helps visitors at the school.

sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect. The principal is too busy for typing.
Incorrect. Prefects are student leaders.
Incorrect. The librarian manages the library.
Correct. The secretary performs these administrative duties.

enonce.texte:
Read the school scenario below and answer the questions that follow using the vocabulary from the lesson.

Scenario: At Government High School Molyko, the first term has just started. John arrives late to school without a proper uniform. His father lost his job, so John hasn't paid his fees yet. The Principal, Mrs. Eyango, the Bursar, Mr. Njei, and the Discipline Master, Mr. Tanto, are discussing the situation.

questions.texte: Which school authority is responsible for collecting John's school fees? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: The Bursar (Mr. Njei) is responsible for collecting school fees. The text states that the Bursar is the financial officer of the school.

questions.texte: John is not wearing the correct uniform. Which authority will most likely handle this infraction? Explain why.
questions.reponse: The Discipline Master (Mr. Tanto) will handle this. The Discipline Master is responsible for maintaining order and punishing student misbehavior, including uniform violations.

questions.texte: Based on the scenario, what is the main difference between the roles of the Principal and the Secretary?
questions.reponse: The Principal (Mrs. Eyango) is responsible for the overall management, discipline, and academic affairs of the school. The Secretary handles administrative support tasks like answering calls, typing letters, and managing files. The Principal makes decisions; the Secretary executes administrative tasks.

questions.texte: Imagine you are the Principal. Formulate a plan to help John continue his education despite his father losing his job. Mention two different school authorities you would involve.
questions.reponse: As Principal, I would: 1) Ask the Bursar to set up a payment plan or a scholarship for John so he can pay fees gradually. 2) Ask the Discipline Master to provide a spare uniform from the school's collection while also talking to John about the importance of appearance, so he doesn't feel humiliated.
